summary: Tight high‑energy Greek run with rippin Deal solo & flawless jams
blurb: The show delivers a scorching first set highlighted by Jack Straw, Deal and a tight Lazy Lightning > Supplication jam. Jerry’s guitar is on fire throughout, while Phil and the dual drummers lock in with crisp precision. The Charlie Miller soundboard capture offers crystal‑clear audio, making this a must‑listen Greek Theatre performance.
review: May 22, 1982 at Berkeley’s Greek Theatre finds the Dead in peak form, firing on all cylinders from the opening of Jack Straw through a blistering second set that never loses its momentum. Jerry Garcia delivers one of his most rippin Deal solos ever heard and strings together clever, story‑like narratives on Tennessee Jed, while his vocals are described as strong and crystal clear. Phil Lesh’s bass shines with a prominent, clean presence that perfectly matches the tight, confident drumming of Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann, creating a rhythm section that feels "superbly tight" throughout. Brent Mydland adds soulful depth on Never Trust A Woman (Good Times Blues) and his vocal reverb is praised for its warmth. Highlights include an immaculate Lazy Lightning > Supplication transition, a seamless China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue, and a powerful Jack Straw that many reviewers call electrifying. The Charlie Miller SBD master captures the performance with crystal‑clear balance, free of dropouts, delivering a listening experience that feels both intimate and expansive – a definitive document of one of the Dead’s high‑energy early‑’80s runs.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six analyzed recordings carry a positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the tightness, high energy, and standout moments (Deal solo, Jack Straw, Lazy Lightning > Supplication, China Cat Sunflower > Rider). No negative comments about performance appear, just minor recording quirks. Consistent acclaim across multiple sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear Charlie Miller soundboard capture with excellent balance, no dropouts, and highlights of the show’s key moments.

key_highlights: Tight, high‑energy performance that never loses momentum
Rippin Deal solo and flawless Lazy Lightning > Supplication jam
Electrifying Jack Straw and seamless China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition
Crystal‑clear soundboard recording offering an immersive listening experience

band_performance.Jerry: Rippin Deal solo, clever Tennessee Jed narrative, strong vocals, and inspiring guitar work that fires on all cylinders throughout the night.
band_performance.Phil: Prominent, clean bass presence that locks tightly with the drums, delivering "good Phil" moments across the set.
band_performance.Bob: Solid vocal contributions and notable penny‑whistle accents during Space, maintaining strong form throughout.
band_performance.Brent: Soulful delivery on Never Trust A Woman (Good Times Blues) with praised vocal reverb and overall keyboard support.
band_performance.Mickey: Tight, confident drumming that helps drive the high‑energy flow of both sets.
band_performance.Billy: Sharp, cohesive percussion that pairs perfectly with Mickey to create a superbly tight rhythm section.
